content.problemStatement:
There are NNN buckets numbered 111 through NNN. The buckets contain balls; each ball has a color between 111 and KKK. Let's denote the number of balls with color jjj that are initially in bucket iii by ai,ja_{i, j}ai,j‚Äã.

For each iii from 111 to N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 (in this order), someone draws a ball uniformly at random from bucket iii and puts it into bucket i+1i+1i+1, then continues to draw the next ball. After putting a ball in bucket NNN, this person draws a ball, again uniformly at random, from bucket NNN.

For each color from 111 to KKK, find the probability that the ball drawn from bucket NNN has this color.
constraints: 0‚â§ai,j‚â§100 \le a_{i, j} \le 100‚â§ai,j‚Äã‚â§10 for each valid i,ji, ji,j
initially, there is at least one ball in bucket 111
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
NNN lines follow. For each iii (1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N), the iii-th of these lines contains KKK space-separated integers ai,1,ai,2,‚Ä¶,ai,Ka_{i, 1}, a_{i, 2}, \ldots, a_{i, K}ai,1‚Äã,ai,2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,ai,K‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing KKK space-separated real numbers. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these numbers should denote the probability that the last drawn ball has color iii. your answer will be considered correct if absolute or relative error does not exceed 10‚àí610^{-6}10‚àí6
